title: Eva is number one according to Newtype readers
display_title: Eva is number one according to Newtype readers
wikitext:
[]: Eva is number one according to Newtype readers...             Quote Post by Ayanami » Wed Nov 03, 2004 6:13 pm Sometime over the summer, Newtype USA magazine put out a message telling fans to vote for what they thought the #1 anime was. Well, the results are in, and according to fans, Evangelion is it! I just got my November Newtype today, and I was very excited to see that Eva has withstood the test of time (to be deliberately corny). I'll try scanning the article. In addition, the centerfold is a beautiful Yoshiyuki Sadamoto drawing of Rei in her plugsuit, with Unit-01 in the background, and on the other side is a TV stillshot of all three Units ready for battle. I'll see if I can scan that too. So... yay!

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Wed Nov 03, 2004 6:31 pm WOW thats awsome!! Please scan them ASAP!! :P

[]:             Quote Post by MAGI » Wed Nov 03, 2004 6:37 pm Wow! This is beautiful news!

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Wed Nov 03, 2004 7:18 pm interesting. never thought that EVA would win an award in a time like this, so far from its original release date. Cartoon Network ought to put it on the air again. Either that or i need to get anime network for my tv

[]:             Quote Post by Ayanami » Wed Nov 03, 2004 7:59 pm [url]http://img.photobucket.com/albums/v64/Faye-Faye/evap2.jpg[/url] [url]http://img.photobucket.com/albums/v64/Faye-Faye/evap1.jpg[/url] Well, aren't you all in luck. I actually already scanned the pages. But since I didn't have the patience to scan the centerfold, you'll just have to wait a little while. One of the pages didn't come out all the way for some stupid reason, and some of the text got cut off, so I've just rewritten what was on the page here for you. You're welcome. :P --- The history of anime can be neatly divided into two eras: before Evangelion , and after. Up until October 1995, anime had continued to grow as both a mass-market medium and a pop-culture art form. Ten along came a whiny child, an overbearing father, a collection of lanky mecha and an inspired assortment of bizarre alien attackers, all set within a postapocalyptic Tokyo that (on the surface, at least) didn't appear all that different from our world-- and suddenly, everything was different. Neon Genesis Evangelion simultaneously caught the eye of culture critics, hardcore enthusiasts and casual viewers, forever changing the way people see cartoons. Borrowing some of the best elements from past mecha shows and infusing it all with a postmodrn existential and religious brew, Evangelion signaled a revolutionary leap forward for anime. Sure, the series touched upon themes that could spur numerous doctoral theses, bu it was also extraordinarily amusing, packed with intense action, gut-wrenching plot twists, gripping character development and gorgeous artwork. To this day, Evangelion remains one of the top-selling, best-loved, most-debated series ever released. And it's your pick for the greatest anime series of all time. If you've seen it, you already know why it's No. 1. If you haven't, well... what are you waiting for? --- So, again, yay! Oh, and if you're interested, here's the list for the 24 other winners. Psh, like we really care. :P (Kidding) 2. Cowboy Bebop 3. Inuyasha 4. Rurouni Kenshin 5. Trigun 6. Fruits Basket 7. Robotech 8. Ranma 1/2 9. Love Hina (my comment: ONLY nine? I think it deserves better than 9... >.>) 10. Sailor Moon 11. Hellsing 12. FLCL 13. Fushigi Yugi 14. Chobits 15. Akira 16. Tenchi Muyo! 17. Azumanga Daioh (my comment: yay! Although I think Azumanga should have been higher up as well) 18. Initial D 19. Dragon Ball Z 20. Vision of Escaflowne 21. Full Metal Panic! 22. Gundam Seed 23. Wolf's Rain 24. Cardcaptor Sakura 25. Excel Saga (my comment: WTF? It was the 25th best anime? Totally should've gotten a better ranking)

[]:             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Wed Nov 03, 2004 8:41 pm I'm kinda suprised Eva got #1 anime, I expected maybe something else. But why did Inuyasha beat out FLCL? In my opinion Inuyasha has been milked of all it's glory and FLCL is a much better, I think it's a work of art sometimes.

[]:             Quote Post by Gilgamesh » Wed Nov 03, 2004 8:47 pm I put the centerfold up on a wall in my room. Evangelion was the only good choice on that list. I honestly don't take it very seriously. I might have, if Haibane Renmei or Texhnolyze had been on the list. Otherwise it's not really what anime is good, just what anime is "popular."

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Wed Nov 03, 2004 8:48 pm Hah! Eva beat Bepop! Take that you stupid Evangelion hating losers (I've found that everyone who hates evangelion, and has seen cowboy bepop, loves cowboy bepop.) FLCL should have been the 12th WORST title of all time! And I agree with all your comments Ayanami; Saga, Daioh, and Hina all should have been higher. I guess the majory of people who voted were extremely fond of the action anime genre, because the top 5 fit into that genre quite well. (HOW THE HELL DID SAKURA BEAT EXCEL SAGA? I mean I like cardcaptor and all, BUT BETTER THAN EXCEL? Never! Hail Il Palazzo!

[]:             Quote Post by Ayanami » Wed Nov 03, 2004 10:11 pm Gilgamesh wrote: I put the centerfold up on a wall in my room. Evangelion was the only good choice on that list. I honestly don't take it very seriously. I might have, if Haibane Renmei or Texhnolyze had been on the list. Otherwise it's not really what anime is good, just what anime is "popular."

[]:             Quote Post by coff » Wed Nov 03, 2004 10:15 pm I don't know why this is suprising to some of you, given the popularity of Eva. And it hasn't been that long since it first aired. You guys are talking like it's 30 years old.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Wed Nov 03, 2004 10:55 pm welll..its been a lot longer than a lot of others... and if they aired it on CN then it would be soooo censored...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Nov 03, 2004 11:22 pm sadsadshinji wrote: welll..its been a lot longer than a lot of others... and if they aired it on CN then it would be soooo censored...

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Nov 04, 2004 12:12 am Hay some of us do like bebop and eva you know?!?! Their two entilry diffrents ends of the anime spectrum and its easy to see why some people love one and hate another. And as for Eva being populare still face it it like Ghost in The Shell or Akairi somthing that love it or hate it evenry anime fan has sceen at least once.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Nov 04, 2004 1:34 am What a surprise. Bebop, InuYasha, and Trigun. "Well, I saw two episodes of this one, so I'll vote or it." J/K, I like them too.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Thu Nov 04, 2004 7:29 am So Eva is the first,that's very cool.. i'm surprised that Sailor Moon is number 10 on the list and Kenshin 4..

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Nov 04, 2004 9:01 am I'm surprised that there's not GitS on there... Or Gundam, for that matter. But anyway, that poll doesn't mean much to me, being as I didn't vote on it. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Olin of Xephon » Thu Nov 04, 2004 1:47 pm Yeah, Evangelion remains really popular. I'm not too surprised it got top place. Although Inu Yasha is no where on my personal top 25 list, what is it people like about that show so much? Nothing wrong with it, way better then some of the stuff out there, but people LOVE that show. What's up? I agree with the article, it was Evangelion that was responsible for anime becoming as main stream as it is...Not that I've ever met a person who likes Evangelion. (except on the internet of course)

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Thu Nov 04, 2004 3:30 pm Wow some of my favriotes came through! 3. Inuyasha 4. Rurouni Kenshin 5. Trigun 20. Vision of Escaflowne 23. Wolf's Rain ( didnt see the whole seris, but what I saw I liked. That sucks that Dual didnt make the list though> :cry:

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Thu Nov 04, 2004 6:35 pm im kinda suprised that gundam wasn't there though. maybe next time though

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Thu Nov 04, 2004 6:38 pm I lost intrest in gundam after like the 10th one.
